story.iWant: a visual regression testing framework that compares converted presets visually with reference images
story.soThat: I can validate color accuracy beyond parameter comparison and ensure creative intent is preserved across format conversions

tasks.task: Task 1: Select and Prepare Reference Images (AC-1) - 5 high-quality photos covering portrait, landscape, vintage, B&W, HDR scenarios
Task 2: Select Representative Presets (AC-2) - 10 presets (3 subtle, 4 moderate, 3 dramatic) covering exposure, HSL, tone curves, split toning
Task 3: Generate Reference Outputs (AC-2) - Apply presets in Lightroom/NX Studio, export as 16-bit TIFF (Adobe RGB, lossless)
Task 4: Convert Presets and Generate Test Outputs (AC-3) - Convert via Recipe, apply in target application, export with same settings
Task 5: Manual Visual Comparison (AC-4) - Side-by-side comparison with ≥95% visual similarity target
Task 6: Calculate Color Delta E (AC-4) - Use ImageMagick for SSIM, optional Python script for delta E (<5 threshold)
Task 7: Document Known Differences (AC-5) - Identify unmappable features (grain, vignette, split toning limits in NP3)
Task 8: Create Visual Regression Report (AC-7) - Comprehensive report with metrics table, side-by-side images, known differences
Task 9: Update User Documentation - Add visual regression section to README, FAQ with accuracy claims

items.item: At least 5 reference images (portrait, landscape, vintage, B&W, HDR)
High quality (≥12MP, RAW or high-quality JPEG)
Diverse color profiles (skin tones, blue skies, green foliage, sunset reds)
At least 10 representative presets tested (subtle → dramatic range)
Applied to reference images in source application (Lightroom/NX Studio)
Metadata recorded (preset name, source format, application version)
Converted presets applied in target application
Test outputs exported as 16-bit TIFF (matching reference settings)
Same image processing pipeline as reference (no extra adjustments)
Color delta E <5 for critical colors (reds, blues, greens, skin tones)
Delta E thresholds: <1 imperceptible, 1-3 minor, 3-5 noticeable (acceptable), >5 significant
Known unmappable features documented (grain, vignette in NP3)
ImageMagick or similar tool for comparison
Results exported as JSON for tracking
Metrics table (delta E, SSIM for each preset-image combo)
Report updated with each Recipe release

criterion.title: CI/CD Integration (Future Enhancement, Deferred to Post-MVP)

items.item: Automated visual regression tests in CI/CD (if automated)
PR blocked if visual fidelity degrades >10%

doc.title: Epic 6 Technical Specification: Validation & Testing
doc.snippet: Visual regression complements automated testing (Story 6-1) by validating that conversions "look right" beyond parameter equality. Target: ≥95% visual similarity, color delta E <5 for critical colors, at least 5 representative presets tested. Manual process acceptable for MVP; automation deferred to post-MVP.

doc.title: Epic 6 Technical Specification: Validation & Testing
doc.snippet: Manual workflow: (1) Prepare reference images and apply preset in source app, (2) Convert preset via Recipe, (3) Apply converted preset in target app, (4) Compare images using ImageMagick/SSIM, (5) Calculate color delta E, (6) Document results. Each test cycle takes ~30 minutes for 5-10 presets.

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: 95%+ accuracy when users compare converted presets side-by-side with originals. Visual inspection shows minimal color drift. Tone curves, HSL adjustments, and calibration preserve creative intent. This is the core promise of Recipe - conversions must "look right" to photographers.

doc.title: Architecture
doc.snippet: Table-driven tests using 1,501 real sample files from testdata/ validate parameter-level accuracy. Round-trip testing ensures bidirectional conversion fidelity. Visual regression (Story 6-2) complements automated tests by validating color accuracy subjectively - proving conversions preserve creative intent.

doc.title: Epic 6 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Type VisualRegressionResult captures delta E (color difference metric), pass threshold (5.0 for critical colors), and pass/fail status. Delta E calculated using CIEDE2000 formula: <1 imperceptible, 1-3 minor, 3-5 noticeable (acceptable), >5 significant (requires investigation).

doc.title: Story 6-1: Automated Test Suite
doc.snippet: Automated tests (Story 6-1) validate parameter-level accuracy (e.g., Contrast = +15 in both files) but cannot validate "looks right". Visual regression (Story 6-2) complements by proving conversions preserve creative intent when applied to actual photos. Both required for 95%+ accuracy claim.

doc.title: Epic 6 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Some features don't map 1:1 between formats. Grain effect not supported in NP3 (Lightroom feature only). Vignette not available in NP3 Picture Control. Split toning limited in NP3 (only hue, no saturation control). All limitations must be documented with visual impact assessment.

artifact.reason: Core conversion API used to convert presets for visual regression testing. Converts between np3, xmp, lrtemplate formats using hub-and-spoke UniversalRecipe pattern.
NP3 parser extracts parameters from Nikon Picture Control binary format. Visual regression validates these parsed parameters produce correct visual output when applied in NX Studio.
NP3 generator creates binary files from UniversalRecipe. Visual regression validates generated NP3 files load correctly in NX Studio and produce expected visual output.
XMP parser extracts parameters from Lightroom CC presets. Visual regression uses XMP presets as reference source for comparison with converted NP3 output.
XMP generator creates Lightroom presets from UniversalRecipe. Visual regression validates reverse conversion (NP3 → XMP) produces correct visual output.
Lrtemplate parser for Lightroom Classic presets. Visual regression may test lrtemplate → NP3 conversion paths to validate broader format coverage.
913 XMP sample files available for selecting representative presets for visual regression testing. Provides diverse preset library covering subtle to dramatic adjustments.
Round-trip test validates parameter fidelity (tolerance ±1). Visual regression complements this by validating visual output matches expectations when presets applied to photos.
CLI used to convert presets for visual regression testing workflow: `recipe convert testdata/xmp/preset.xmp --to np3 --output test.np3`. Enables batch conversion for test preparation.

dependencies.go: Standard library only (no external dependencies for core conversion); Go 1.24+ for WASM support and enhanced type system

external.tool: Adobe Lightroom CC or Classic (required for applying XMP/lrtemplate presets to reference images)
Nikon NX Studio (required for applying NP3 presets to reference images, free download from Nikon)
ImageMagick (optional, for automated SSIM calculation and visual diff generation)
Python 3.x + PIL + colormath (optional, for automated delta E calculation using CIEDE2000 formula)

testdata.item: High-quality reference photos (5 minimum: portrait, landscape, vintage, B&W, HDR)
Representative presets from testdata/xmp/ (10 presets covering subtle to dramatic adjustments)
All test data committed to repository for reproducibility and community validation

constraints.constraint: Visual regression testing is manual for MVP (automation deferred to post-MVP)
Testing requires access to Adobe Lightroom CC/Classic and Nikon NX Studio applications
Reference images must be high quality (≥12MP) with diverse color profiles
Export settings must be consistent (16-bit TIFF, Adobe RGB, lossless compression)
Delta E <5 threshold for critical colors (reds, blues, greens, skin tones)
Known unmappable features (grain, vignette, split toning limits) must be documented
All test data (images, reference outputs, test outputs) committed to repository
Process is time-intensive (~30 minutes per test cycle for 5-10 presets)
Manual process acceptable because visual validation is inherently subjective
Results must be documented in docs/visual-regression-results.md with side-by-side images
Community must be able to validate results independently (transparent test data)
This story is purely validation and documentation - no code changes to conversion engine
Complements Story 6-1 (automated test suite) which validates parameter-level accuracy

interface.name: converter.Convert
interface.signature: func Convert(input []byte, from, to string) ([]byte, error)
interface.description: Core conversion API used to convert presets for visual regression testing. Stateless, format-agnostic conversion engine.

interface.name: np3.Parse
interface.description: Parses NP3 binary format into UniversalRecipe. Visual regression validates parsed parameters produce correct visual output.

interface.name: np3.Generate
interface.description: Generates NP3 binary from UniversalRecipe. Visual regression validates generated files load correctly in NX Studio.

interface.name: xmp.Parse
interface.description: Parses XMP XML format into UniversalRecipe. Visual regression uses XMP as reference source for comparison.

interface.name: xmp.Generate
interface.description: Generates XMP XML from UniversalRecipe. Visual regression validates reverse conversion (NP3 → XMP) accuracy.

interface.name: ImageMagick compare
interface.description: Optional automated SSIM (Structural Similarity Index) calculation. Returns float 0-1 where >0.95 is excellent similarity.

interface.name: colormath delta_e_cie2000
interface.description: Optional automated color delta E calculation using CIEDE2000 formula. Threshold: <5 for critical colors acceptable.

tests.standards: Visual regression testing is manual for MVP and complements the automated test suite (Story 6-1). Testing framework: (1) Reference Preparation: Apply presets in source application (Lightroom/NX Studio), export as 16-bit TIFF, (2) Conversion: Use Recipe CLI to convert presets to target format, (3) Test Output: Apply converted presets in target application, export with identical settings, (4) Comparison: Side-by-side visual assessment + optional automated metrics (SSIM, delta E), (5) Documentation: Record results in docs/visual-regression-results.md with metrics table and images. Quality gates: Visual similarity ≥95%, delta E <5 for critical colors, SSIM >0.95. All test data committed to repository for community validation and reproducibility. This story creates NEW test infrastructure but does not modify existing code. Pure validation and documentation effort.

locations.location: scripts/visual-regression/ (optional automation scripts for SSIM and delta E)

ideas.idea: Select 5 diverse reference images - Validate images are high quality (≥12MP, RAW or high-quality JPEG) - Verify coverage of color profiles (skin tones, blue skies, green foliage, sunset reds) - Confirm images documented in testdata/visual-regression/README.md - Test: Attempt to parse images in photo viewer, verify metadata shows resolution and color space

Generate reference outputs from 10 representative presets - Validate presets cover intensity range (3 subtle, 4 moderate, 3 dramatic) - Verify export settings are consistent (16-bit TIFF, Adobe RGB, lossless) - Confirm metadata recorded (preset name, source format, application version) - Test: Open reference TIFF in photo editor, verify bit depth and color space match specification

Generate test outputs from converted presets - Validate conversion succeeds for all test presets via Recipe CLI - Verify converted presets load correctly in target application (no errors) - Confirm export settings match reference (16-bit TIFF, Adobe RGB, lossless) - Test: Compare file sizes - reference and test should be similar if same image and settings

Calculate image comparison metrics - Manual visual comparison: Open reference and test side-by-side, assess ≥95% similarity - Optional automated SSIM: Run ImageMagick compare, verify >0.95 threshold - Optional delta E: Run Python script, verify <5 for critical colors - Test: Spot-check metrics on known identical images (should be delta E ~0, SSIM ~1.0)

Document known differences - Identify presets with grain effect (XMP → NP3 loses grain, NP3 doesn't support) - Identify presets with vignette (XMP → NP3 loses vignette, not in Picture Control) - Identify presets with split toning (NP3 has limited support, only hue not saturation) - Test: Apply preset with grain in Lightroom, convert to NP3, apply in NX Studio - verify grain is missing

Create comprehensive visual regression report - Validate report includes summary metrics (excellent/good/acceptable/poor breakdown) - Verify side-by-side images embedded (5-10 examples) - Confirm metrics table complete (delta E, SSIM for each preset-image combo) - Test: Open report in Markdown viewer, verify all images render and tables format correctly

Validate community can reproduce results - Verify all reference images committed to repository - Verify all presets used are in testdata/ - Verify export settings documented - Test: Follow documentation from scratch, attempt to reproduce one reference output

Performance validation - Measure time for manual visual regression cycle (5-10 presets) - Target: ~30 minutes per cycle (acceptable for manual process) - Document time breakdown: preset application, export, comparison - Test: Time actual workflow execution, compare to estimate
